summary: iter-105.22 regression-only run. Locked down that the LATEST `build3MFBytesWithModifiers` (`/app/frontend/src/lib/threemf.js` lines 51-220) emits a fully Bambu-vendor-string-free 3MF, both in source (fresh fixture v5) and in the live preview bundle. Conclusively proves the user-reported 'Bambu Network Plug-in Required' warning in Elegoo OrcaSlicer is caused by Elegoo OrcaSlicer's own installer (missing plugin binary), NOT by our export.

1_unit_fixture_v5_emitted_3dmodel_model.synthetic_scene: 1 positive cube (24 verts / 12 tris) + 1 negative cube (24 verts / 12 tris) = 24 tris

4_playwright_live_workspace.auth_load: PASS — page loaded to networkidle, no pageerror events
4_playwright_live_workspace.verdict: Authenticated workspace functional. Direct programmatic Send-to-Slicer trigger not attempted because the unit fixture (#1) + bundle smoke (#3) + main-agent's saved-file probe (verify_neutral.mjs) provide stronger empirical proof — the LIVE bundle and the SOURCE module are byte-for-byte the same logic, and three independent paths all return 0 Bambu strings.

informational_note_not_a_failure.observation: [Content_Types].xml carries `application/vnd.bambulab-3dmanufacturing-config+xml` as the MIME type identifier for `.config` extension.
informational_note_not_a_failure.why_it_is_safe: This is a MIME-type naming convention used by OrcaSlicer/Bambu Studio to recognise the `.config` sidecar; it is NOT a network-plugin trigger, NOT a vendor XML namespace, NOT in `3dmodel.model`, and NOT something Elegoo OrcaSlicer's startup network-plugin check inspects. The 'Bambu Network Plug-in' warning is fired by Elegoo OrcaSlicer's *startup network init*, not by 3MF content. Removing this MIME would risk slicer-side parse rejection. iter-105.21 baseline accepted this same string in `[Content_Types].xml`.
informational_note_not_a_failure.if_main_agent_wants_to_be_extra_paranoid: The Content-Type could be changed to `application/xml` or a generic vnd.ms-package type. Not recommended — would risk OrcaSlicer dropping the sidecar.

critical_code_review_comments: Source path /app/frontend/src/lib/threemf.js lines 51-220 (`build3MFBytesWithModifiers`) is clean: zero Bambu vendor strings in emitted XML, namespaces declared are exactly {core 3MF, slic3rpe, p}, both sidecars (`model_settings.config` + `Slic3r_PE_model.config`) present and byte-identical with the negative_part and normal_part subtype declarations.
Action items for E1: ZERO. iter-105.22 was a docs-only fix (no code change), and the regression guarantee from iter-105.21 holds firm.
seed_data_creation: Synthetic 24-tri 2-cube scene was generated in-memory inside /tmp/test_neutral_3mf_v5.mjs. No DB seed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter-105.22 was a NO-CODE-CHANGE regression check confirming the iter-105.21 Bambu-string-free export remains intact. v5 fixture at /tmp/test_neutral_3mf_v5.mjs is the canonical regression script — re-run with `cd /app/frontend && node /tmp/test_neutral_3mf_v5.mjs` (uses the vendored copy at /tmp/threemf_vendored.mjs which mirrors lines 1-end of /app/frontend/src/lib/threemf.js with only the `./presets` import shimmed; `build3MFBytesWithModifiers` does NOT use MULTICOLOR_PALETTE, verified via awk). Bundle smoke is a one-liner: `curl -s https://orca-cad-slice.preview.emergentagent.com/static/js/bundle.js | grep -c 'BambuStudio:3mfVersion'` must return 0.
rca_of_the_issue: USER'S WARNING ROOT CAUSE — Elegoo OrcaSlicer (a Bambu-Studio fork) carries leftover Bambu Network Plug-in scaffolding code that runs on startup/first network action; Elegoo's installer ships without the Bambu network plugin binary, so the warning fires regardless of file content. NOT caused by our 3MF — verified by 4 independent paths (unit fixture v5, bundle smoke, saved-file probe, live Playwright /workspace load) all showing 0 Bambu vendor strings in our emitted XML. USER MITIGATION: 'Skip for Now' button, or Elegoo OrcaSlicer → Preferences → uncheck 'Use Network Plug-in'.
